summary: Great audience recording captures a fire‑filled ’73 performance with standout renditions of Cumberland Blues, Here Comes Sunshine and China > Rider.
review: The show is consistently praised for its high‑energy ’73 vibe, flawless playing and many highlight moments – especially the opening “Cumberland Blues,” the sunshine‑laden “Here Comes Sunshine” and the soaring “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider.”  The audience (AUD) source is described as clear, lively and even a bit “muffled” in places, with a mono second set that doesn’t detract from the overall listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Nearly all reviews are 4‑5 stars and rave about both performance and sound; the show is described as exceptional while the audience recording, though not pristine, is judged good enough to be highly enjoyable.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and loud with a lively ambience; some reviewers note slight muffling and the second set being mono, but overall the AUD capture is praised for putting the listener ‘there.’

standout_songs: The Other One (mentioned as first Dark Star experience)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight a flawless first set brimming with energy and a second set that, despite being mono, maintains the momentum – the concert is described as a nonstop jam fest with no weak moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised as delivering perhaps his best ’73 playing; “Garcia on fire,” “Jerry just plays his ass off.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned in context of vocals – “Donna and the boys are on vocally” and a note that Phil’s vocal on ‘Box of Rain’ isn’t what it used to be, but no criticism of his bass work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill is noted as possibly needing more volume, but overall drums sit well in the mix.
